member.name: Alex Sobel
member.role: MP for Leeds Central and Headingley
member.synopsis: Alex Sobel is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Leeds Central and Headingley , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential benefits of requiring food labels to declare levels of potassium and phosphate content.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The labelling of packaged foods and drinks plays an important role in helping individuals access the information they need to make informed choices about the food they buy. Food labelling legislation sets out the information that must be displayed on a pr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Government: Data Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, when the Department will publish a version of the GOV.UK One Login Data Protection Impact Assessment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave on 15 April 2026 to Question UIN 125374.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Child Trust Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what her policy is on introducing automatic release of funds from unclaimed adult-owned Child Trust Funds.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is aware of proposals for the automatic release of funds in unclaimed matured Child Trust Fund (CTF) accounts. These savings belong to the account holders and are held by private sector providers. The Government does not have the authority ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Sanitary Products
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether the period product scheme will continue within schools in 2026-27.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Nobody should have to miss out on education due to their period, which is why the government provides access to free period products to girls and women who need them in their place of study. Schools and colleges are able to order free period products via ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Work and Pensions: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, when his Department plans to respond to outstanding casework queries from the hon. Member for Leeds Central and Headingley dated between December 2025 and April 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department recognises there have been some delays to complaint responses due to increased volumes of complex complaints requiring more detailed investigation. To address this, the Department has deployed additional resources to complaints and correspo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Work and Pensions: Correspondence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether he has any plans to offer engagement calls with hon. Members' offices to resolve ongoing cases expediently, such as the ones offered by the Home Office.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Work and Pensions recognise the importance of timely resolution of constituents’ cases and remains committed to supporting Members of Parliament and their offices in progressing enquiries. The Department already provides channels for MP...

writtenQuestions.answerText: We continue to raise with Shell, including at Ministerial level, the importance of action to address the risks of pollution from oil production in the Niger Delta, in line with international standards. The regulation of companies within Nigeria and the go...

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, who are able to apply for Local Power Plans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The DESNZ/GBE Local Power Plan is designed to engage a wide range of stakeholders and participants including community energy organisations, local authorities as well as investors and lenders. The initial entry route for those seeking support is typically...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Disabled Students' Allowances: Assistive Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact on disabled students, including on educational outcomes and equality, of proposals to restrict funding for specialist assistive software through D
writtenQuestions.answerText: Supporting disabled students to access and succeed in higher education is a government priority. Disabled Students’ Allowance (DSA) provides support to remove disability-related barriers to learning, enabling students to access academic opportunities. DSA...

writtenQuestions.heading: Disabled Students' Allowances: Assistive Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what evidence her Department used to conclude that free university tools can adequately replace specialist assistive technology funded by the Disabled Students Allowance to support disabled students with their
writtenQuestions.answerText: Supporting disabled students to access and succeed in higher education is a government priority. Disabled Students’ Allowance (DSA) provides support to remove disability-related barriers to learning, enabling students to access academic opportunities. DSA...

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Licences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking to reduce delays in processing driving licence applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA)’s online services are the quickest and easiest way to apply for and renew a driving licence. There are no delays in successful online applications and customers should receive their driving licence within a f...

writtenQuestions.heading: Timber: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department distinguishes between sustainably managed commercial forestry and deforestation-linked timber imports.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Global Environmental Impacts of Consumption indicator, funded by Defra, estimates that UK consumption of wood products is linked to circa 8,000 hectares of deforestation in 2023, associated with circa 0.4m tonnes of CO2 emissions. The UK has a robust ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Timber: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of sustainably sourced imported timber on the UK’s net zero and decarbonisation objectives.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We recognise the important role timber can play in supporting Net Zero, including through reducing embodied emissions in construction and storing carbon. The Government relaunched a more ambitious Timber in Construction Roadmap in February 2025 to increas...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Disaster Relief
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the adequacy of the eligibility criteria and membership structure of the Rapid Response Facility (RRF) under the Humanitarian Respo
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Rapid Response Facility has not been used since 2019 and there are no plans to revive its use. Alternative mechanisms, such as the Start Fund have provided a more efficient route for providing funding to NGOs, and currently has a membership of 145 NGO...

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthwatch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, in the context of the proposals to abolish Healthwatch, how he will ensure that people who are less likely to engage with health services directly, including those experiencing health inequalities,
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Patient Experience Directorate in the Department of Health and Social Care will focus on groups less likely to engage with health and care services. Statutory guidance published by the Department will make it clear that integrated care boards and loca...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Healthwatch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of abolishing local Healthwatch on public trust in providing feedback on health and care services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: An impact assessment has been undertaken and published alongside the introduction of the Health Bill 2026. It outlines the non-monetised benefits, including the streamlining of the landscape and legislation, avoiding duplication of activity, and increased...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Allied Health Professions: Leeds Central and Headingley
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to recruit and retain allied health professionals in Leeds Central and Headingley constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to making the National Health Service the best place to work to support and retain our hardworking and dedicated healthcare professionals. As set out in the 10-Year Health Plan, we are working closely with NHS England, employer...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services and Social Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether guidance will be issued to Integrated Care Boards and local authorities on how to deliver independent and inclusive engagement with local communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On behalf of my Rt Hon. Friend, the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, the Department will set the direction, issuing clear guidance to integrated care boards and local authorities on obtaining and actioning the views of local people on health...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

A director (unpaid) of Transform Labour Ltd (formally Transform Europe (Campaign) Ltd). This is a dormant company, limited by guarantee, which applied for dissolution on 28 June 2023. Date interest arose: 2 July 2019 (Registered 28 July 2023)
A visiting Parliamentary Fellow at St Anthonys College, Oxford. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 4 December 2023 (Registered 14 March 2024)
Vice Chair of the Climate and Nature Crisis Caucus. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 15 July 2025 (Registered 11 September 2025)
Trustee of Leeds International Aid Fund. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 30 October 2025 (Registered 6 November 2025)
Member of Executive Committee of the British Group of the Inter Parliamentary Union Date interest arose: 26 November 2025 (Registered 28 November 2025)
